description: Formalized decision rules and context detection priorities for Apache Airflow agent skills. These rules guide agent decision-making and can be audited/versioned.

context_detection.description: Priority chain for detecting execution context. Agents check in order.

priority.description: User has explicitly set AIRFLOW_HOME environment variable
priority.implies: User wants to work with this Airflow instance
Running inside Docker — use breeze commands
Assume host environment — use native tools

execution_decision_tree.description: Decision logic for choosing between host and Breeze execution

rules.title: Prefer native execution for speed
rules.rationale: Native execution is 3-5x faster, better IDE integration

rules.title: Fall back to Breeze if native fails with dependency error
rules.condition: Command fails with 'missing system dependencies' or similar
rules.rationale: Breeze has all dependencies pre-installed; avoids host pollution

rules.title: Use Breeze for container-specific tests
rules.condition: Tests require Kubernetes, Docker, or container orchestration
rules.rationale: These tests require Docker daemon and K8s cluster

rules.title: Never try to predict failure — react to actual errors
rules.action: Start with fast path; if it fails with system dependency error, retry with fallback
rules.rationale: Prevents false positives and unnecessary Breeze overhead

fallback_error_patterns.description: Error patterns that trigger fallback to Breeze when detected

command_ordering.description: Order of command execution matters for agent accuracy. Rules should come BEFORE examples.

rule_ordering.description: When to use which execution path
rule_ordering.example: Use uv for fast path; use Breeze if dependencies missing
Scheduler never runs user code; workers never access metadata DB
Always format with ruff; use dedicated exception types
Cover success, failure, and edge cases
Run a test: uv run --project <PROJECT> pytest path/to/test.py::TestClass::test_method -xvs

skill_extraction.description: Rules for extracting and validating skills from contributing-docs

constraints: Skill ID must be unique across all skills
Host and breeze commands must not be identical (indicates copy-paste error)
Commands must have valid shell syntax (balanced quotes and braces)
Each skill must be < 200 tokens (approximately 150 words)
error_handling: Raise SkillValidationError if required field is missing
Raise SkillDuplicateError if workflow ID is duplicated
Print warning if command syntax is suspicious
Print warning if skill size exceeds 150 tokens

agent_priming.description: How to structure documentation for optimal agent comprehension

guidelines.rationale: Agents perform better when decision logic appears before concrete examples
guidelines.example: AGENTS.md should list '1. Use uv for fast path' BEFORE showing specific uv commands

guidelines.rationale: Agent context windows are limited; skills should summarize, not duplicate docs
guidelines.threshold: Skill should be < 40% of source documentation section size

guidelines.rationale: Agents handle structured conditionals better than long descriptions
guidelines.format: Use ASCII art decision trees or bullet-lists with if/then structure

guidelines.benefit: Agents can find what they need without wading through all details

ci_validation.description: Automated checks that ensure skills remain current and valid

checks.name: docs_currency
checks.what: Test that all skill commands are syntactically valid and reference valid executables
checks.failure_mode: Fails CI if any skill command is invalid

checks.name: skill_conciseness
checks.what: Verify each skill is concise relative to source documentation
checks.failure_mode: Fails if any skill exceeds 200 tokens or average exceeds 150 tokens

checks.name: skill_extraction
checks.what: Detect drift between committed skills.json and contributing-docs/*.rst
checks.failure_mode: Fails if skills.json is out of sync with source documentation
